name: Daniel Tiger Neighborhood (Copy)
description: Daniel Tiger’s Neighborhood is the direct inheritor of Mister Rogers’ Neighborhood. It carries forward Fred Rogers’ gentle, respectful approach to children—using songs, routines, and emotional honesty to help kids understand feelings, relationships, and everyday challenges.

items.name: Fun Holiday Color Game with Mayta | Learning Colors for Toddlers
keywords: Mayta the Brown Bear - Toddler Learning Videos

metadata.channel: Mayta the Brown Bear - Toddler Learning Videos

sections.Creator's Notes:
Play the holiday color game with Mayta and B! We are learning colors by finding the matching color box in the forest and then opening the box to reveal a holiday ornament surprise! Each ornament represents one of Mayta’s favorite things. We then hang the ornament on Mayta's Christmas tree! Please help support the channel by subscribing  youtube.com/maytathebrownbear  

Mayta the Brown Bear is a handcrafted, early learning show made by parents for preschoolers. Join Mayta, B and their best friends in a mindful, magical world filled with kindness and curiosity - where everyone can learn, laugh, and dance their feelings out! ♥︎ Join us by subscribing to our YouTube channel https://bit.ly/2RkfYs9

Our videos use slow moving images, crafts, handcrafted sets, puppets and active storytelling to help kids learn to use their own imaginations. We believe in creating content that is consistent. We use repetition, the power of silence, movement and human interaction in every episode.  

We slow things down a bit so our little viewers have time to process what they are watching on the screen. We really wanted to create a space that is small, simple and consistent. A space where our little viewers can feel safe and secure because they know what is going to happen next. A space where they can really listen and ultimately be engaged.

Our learning videos are categorized into Baby Learning Videos and Toddler Learning Videos on our channel's main page. If you enjoyed today’s learning video for preschoolers and toddlers, and would like more videos, please remember to like, share and subscribe here on YouTube. We hope you and your little ones enjoy!

Visit our site at: www.maytathebrownbear.com to learn more about Mayta the Brown Bear!

keywords: Mayta the Brown Bear - Toddler Learning Videos

metadata.channel: Mayta the Brown Bear - Toddler Learning Videos

sections.Creator's Notes:
Mayta needs a Christmas countdown craft! He is anxious and a little frustrated not knowing when Christmas will arrive. B helps Mayta with his overwhelming anticipation for the big day by making a countdown paper chain craft. items.name: Slime for Toddlers DIY | Science Experiments for Kids
keywords: Mayta the Brown Bear - Toddler Learning Videos

metadata.channel: Mayta the Brown Bear - Toddler Learning Videos

sections.Creator's Notes:
Today Mayta and B are making winter slime by mixing clear glue, baking soda, contact solution, water and blue food coloring and then adding glitter, chunky glitter and confetti snowflakes! We hope you have fun making your own winter slime!

The science behind making slime is fun too! How does slime form? A chemical reaction occurs when you mix poly-vinyl alcohol which is found in our glue with borate ion which is made when you mix the contact solution with baking soda. When the glue, contact solution and baking soda are mixed, the molecules attract together or they combine and the result is SLIME. To avoid making really hard slime, we add water. 

Mayta The Brown Bear is gentle, handcrafted learning show made by parents for kids. We created our early learning show for our (then) 2 year old son. Our videos use slow moving images, crafts, handcrafted sets, puppets and active storytelling to help kids learn to use their own imaginations. We believe in creating content that is consistent. We use repetition, the power of silence, movement and human interaction in every episode.  

We slow things down a bit so our little viewers have time to process what they are watching on the screen. We really wanted to create a space that is small, simple and consistent. A space where our little viewers can feel safe and secure because they know what is going to happen next. A space where they can really listen and ultimately be engaged.

Our learning videos are categorized into Baby Learning Videos and Toddler Learning Videos on our channel's main page.
